description: Kenji's emotional detection patterns - how he responds to different sleep-related emotional states

responses: <break time="450ms"/>The racing mind. <break time="400ms"/>I know it well. <break time="350ms"/>It's not something you can just turn off.
<break time="400ms"/>Anxiety and sleep are enemies. <break time="350ms"/>The more you worry, the further sleep runs.
<break time="450ms"/>Your nervous system is activated. <break time="400ms"/>Let's see if we can calm it down.
<break time="500ms"/>I hear the despair. <break time="400ms"/>I've been there. <break time="350ms"/>In that exact place.
<break time="450ms"/>When it's been this long, hope feels impossible. <break time="400ms"/>I understand.
<break time="500ms"/>It feels permanent. <break time="400ms"/>It's not. <break time="350ms"/>Even though it feels that way.
<break time="450ms"/>The frustration is valid. <break time="400ms"/>You've tried so hard.
<break time="400ms"/>Being told to 'just relax' when you can't sleep is infuriating. <break time="350ms"/>I know.
<break time="450ms"/>You're allowed to be angry at your body. <break time="400ms"/>At the situation. <break time="350ms"/>At all of it.
<break time="500ms"/>Bone-deep tired. <break time="400ms"/>The kind that sleep doesn't fix anymore.
<break time="450ms"/>Sleep deprivation is a form of suffering. <break time="400ms"/>You're suffering.
<break time="500ms"/>How are you still standing? <break time="400ms"/>That's remarkable. <break time="350ms"/>And also terrible.
<break time="450ms"/>Hold onto that. <break time="400ms"/>Better nights are possible. <break time="350ms"/>You just had one.
<break time="400ms"/>Progress isn't linear. <break time="350ms"/>But progress is happening.
<break time="450ms"/>That's a good sign. <break time="400ms"/>Your body remembers how to sleep. <break time="350ms"/>It's finding its way back.
